entities.name: CAJANUS ACUTIFOLIUS
entities.description: A wild species of pigeonpea endemic to Australia that is cross incompatible with cultivated species.

entities.name: PIGEONPEA
entities.description: The cultivated species Cajanus cajan of Indian origin.

entities.name: AUSTRALIA
entities.description: The region where wild species of pigeonpea are endemic.

entities.name: INDIA
entities.description: The region of origin for cultivated pigeonpea species.

entities.name: INTERSPECIFIC POLLINATION
entities.description: The process of pollinating different species to produce hybrids.

entities.name: HYBRID SEEDS
entities.description: Seeds resulting from interspecific pollinations, described as semi-shrivelled in this context.

entities.name: F1 PLANTS
entities.description: The first filial generation of plants derived from hybrid seeds.

entities.name: BACKCROSSING
entities.description: The process of crossing hybrid plants back to the male parent C. cajan.

entities.name: BC1 PLANTS
entities.description: The first backcross generation plants obtained after saving aborting embryos in vitro.

entities.name: IN VITRO
entities.description: The laboratory technique used to save aborting embryos to obtain BC1 plants.

entities.name: IN VIVO
entities.description: The natural environment where aborting seeds did not germinate.

entities.name: MEIOTIC PAIRING
entities.description: The process of chromosome pairing during meiosis, observed as normal in BC1 plants.

entities.name: POLLEN FERTILITY
entities.description: The ability of pollen to fertilize, observed as low in BC1 plants.

entities.name: EMBRYO ABORTION
entities.description: The failure of embryo development leading to non-germinating seeds.

entities.name: WILD SPECIES CYTOPLASM
entities.description: The cytoplasm from wild species suspected to cause embryo abortion and low pollen fertility.
